description: Want to view the winter wonderland from a comfortable spot? Bernina Express’s 122-kilometer-long train ride allows you to soak up all of the snow-covered Alps, glacier lakes, narrow valleys, and the Graubünden mountains.
I took yet another tour around the Bellevue Palace before heading up to Altes Tramdepot for a pint with a view.
The town hosts the famous art festival, Artisti di Strada, in May and is a great destination for art lovers.

This is one of the oldest chocolate shops in Geneva. Opened in 1939, this family-run chocolaterie features chocolate made by hand. It’s known for its black truffles which are hand-coated, something that is rare these days.
A cozy accommodation for budget travelers, offering free wifi, breakfast buffet, free coffee, tea, and hot chocolate, as well as a large kitchen and help with booking trips and activities.

Bains is an artificial peninsula that's surrounded by a beach, it's the perfect spot to spend the afternoon, and the entrance fee is only CHF 50, you'll get access to the saunas, the La Bouvette des Bains restaurant, the beach and you can book a massage for CHF 60.

You can then venture in and casually walk around the old town, take in a vast array of food and drink options, check out the hipster town of Carouge, lay on the beach in Bains Des Paquis, or attend the Plainpalais Market.

Bains is an artificial peninsula surrounded by a busy beach. During the hot summer days, it’s a great spot to sunbathe and swim. There’s also a zip-line above the water that you can ride. If you’re looking for more of a relaxing experience, you can spend time in the baths and saunas.
Bains is an artificial peninsula surrounded by a busy beach. During the hot summer days, it’s a great spot to sunbathe and swim.
A one-of-a-kind Swiss chalet hostel offering free breakfast, wifi, hot tub, and hammock access, with daily events to connect travelers.

I planned to visit the US embassy in Switzerland to get more passport pages added, then head to Basel to fly out.

The city hosts a Christmas market in the winter and is a great destination for holiday shoppers.
A bar located in the Bellevue Palace Bern hotel where I had dinner and a cocktail. They offer a special menu item called the Bellevue Lady Burger with only 125g of meat.

It's absolutely stunning. After checking into the hotel, I decided to stroll through town and discovered just how enchanting it was.

A Ladylike Stay at Bellevue Palace Bern

A gorgeous and historic hotel in the heart of Bern with a grandeur and beauty that left me awestruck. The stained glass ceiling flooded the foyer with natural light and the smell of fresh cut flowers tickled my nose. The hotel has character and classic elegance in every detail. I was greeted with a 5-star smile and escorted to my room. The room had a selection of women's magazines, including the latest issue of VOGUE, and a double-doored balcony with an incredible view of the River Aare and the distant Bernese Alps. The hotel also features heated bathroom floors and a variety of amenities catering to female guests, including facial cleansing wipes, make-up, toiletries, hair spray, curling tongs, hair straighteners, silk stockings & tights, and yoga mats for hire.
Head west to Lake Maggiore and you’ll be trying to figure out if you’ve stepped into Italy itself or head to the capital, Bellinzona, to visit the three UNESCO world heritage site castles.

As soon as you read UNESCO Heritage Site, then you know it’s a place you need to see. On the UNESCO list since 1983, Bern’s Old City has brought in visitors to walk its medieval street plan, witness the city’s most recognizable symbol — the clock tower — and stroll down the charming Kramgasse street.

A mountain range that can be seen from the hotel room balcony, offering a breathtaking view.

A mountain range in Switzerland that offered a beautiful view from my hotel room balcony. The view drifted over the River Aare to the distant Bernese Alps and was breathtaking.

place: Cafe Restaurant du Parc Des Bastions
description:
The late 19th-century pavilion offers some of the best breakfast in the city and is the perfect spot to kick off your short time in Geneva, with a great indoor seating area or a beautiful outdoor terrace if you're looking to soak up some sun.

This late 19th-century pavilion is another relaxing spot for breakfast. There is a grand indoor seating area or an outdoor terrace for those warm days.

The best part about Cafe de La Place is that you will always experience something different every time you visit. The menu varies depending on seasonal ingredients. The French cuisine has a southern European influence that will leave you wanting more.

If you're a foodie, don't miss out on trying fondue in Geneva, Cafe du Soleil is highly recommended for a late afternoon snack.

You can’t visit Switzerland without trying cheese fondue! Café du Soleil is one of the top recommendations for fondue.

Café Calla is a restaurant located in the Mandarin Oriental Geneva. It serves French-inspired menu, including salads, ceviche, pasta, land and sea plates, along with a wide variety of wines from its 1500-bottle cellar. The restaurant is a great spot for a faster-paced meal or Sunday brunch.
Carouge can be considered Geneva’s bohemian or “hipster” neighborhood. You won’t find any large chains but rather you’ll find plenty of local restaurants, cafes, and bars.
Carouge is considered Geneva's bohemian neighborhood, you'll find lots of beautiful restaurants, bars, and cafes, making it the perfect place to wind down after a day at the lake.
Cathedral St. Pierre is one of the highlights of Old Town Geneva.
The town hosts the International Balloon Festival in January and is a great destination for families.

The Château Chillon, located at Lake Geneva, overlooks the stunning mountain ranges around. As the most popular historical buildings to visit in Switzerland, you’ll spend plenty of time roaming around the grounds and be in awe of the beauty.
The high-ceilinged industrial cafe serves pastries, coffee, salads, and sandwiches. They also have homemade hot chocolate to help warm you up during the cooler months.
One of the dramatic mountains surrounding Interlaken.
Visit the Flower Clock if you have extra time in your Geneva itinerary.
The Funky Chocolate Club offers chocolate treats and chocolate-making classes where you can craft your own chocolate bar by hand.

Geneva is the perfect place to begin your adventure in Switzerland, with endless amounts of chocolate, an incredibly rich history, and one of the most beautiful mountain ranges in the world.

If peace is what you are looking for, you’ll find it in Geneva. Surrounded by the Alps, with plenty of things to see and do, it’s the perfect little Switzerland city you should experience. Firstly, if views are what you want, Lake Geneva is where you need to be. We are talking absolutely stunning full panoramic views of the Swiss Alps.

The city hosts the Fête de la Musique in June and is a great destination for music lovers.
Geneva's historic Old Town is a cultural and touristic district located near the Mandarin Oriental Geneva. It is one of the many attractions that can be accessed from the hotel.

What’s considered Switzerland’s most beautiful village is also a great opportunity to step out of the metropolitan area and get back into nature. While it is very well known for being a fantastic skiing destination, there is plenty to do that doesn’t involve the slopes—especially when you consider the other seasons! Lake Bachalpsee, also known as the blue jewel, is just one of many mountaintops’ water features that your eyes and senses will greatly appreciate.

A cheesy name that may be familiar to you, Gruyere is a common cheese often used for Switzerland’s famous fondues and other melty dishes. It is also the reason why many people visit the famous town to tour its cheese factory. While you can also visit its 13th-century castle and Saint-Germain Castle, small arts regional museum, and the Tibet Museum which is also home to a small church, the cheese is where it’s at.

The town is known for its delicious fondue and is a great destination for foodies.

This luxurious five-star hotel is a top member of the Leading Hotels of the World. It features 90 rooms, 18 of which are suites with an incredible view of the lake. You’ll find beautiful chandeliers inside along large windows.

Hotel Les Arcades is an excellent option if you're traveling to Geneva on a budget, it's only a 20-minute walk from the city center and has all the basics you'll need.

This budget-friendly hotel is a 20-minute walk from Geneva city center. All rooms include wifi, cable TV, a DVD player, and a large window with a view.

This grand hotel has been welcoming royalty and celebrities since 1872. On clear days, you can see the snow-capped Mont-Blanc and the Jet d’Eau fountain. You will be welcomed with brass finishes and vases full of long-stemmed roses, making the rooms feel rich and historic.

A picture-perfect destination nestled between emerald-colored lakes and snowcapped mountains, Interlaken has long been admired as a dream getaway.

Interlaken is an incredible place for a winter getaway with tons of adventurous opportunities.

The town is a great destination for outdoor activities and is surrounded by beautiful scenery, including the Jungfrau mountain.
Jet d'Eau is a famous landmark in Geneva, located near the Mandarin Oriental Geneva. It is one of the many attractions that can be accessed from the hotel.

One of the dramatic mountains surrounding Interlaken.

The mountain is a great destination for hiking and outdoor activities and offers beautiful scenery.
Jungfrau Mountain is the highest point in Europe, offering a panoramic view of the Alps.

The La Bouvette des Bains restaurant is super affordable and delicious, and is located at the Bains des Paquis.

Lake Bachalpsee, also known as the blue jewel, is just one of many mountaintops’ water features that your eyes and senses will greatly appreciate.

Lake Brienz is one of the two stunning lakes that the city of Interlaken is sandwiched between, with beautiful cliffs, waterfalls, and magical villages.

The lake is a great destination for outdoor activities and is surrounded by beautiful scenery.

No trip to Geneva would be complete without spending a few hours exploring Lake Geneva, you can hire a paddleboard, go kayaking or buy a fishing permit for only 10 CHF.

We are talking absolutely stunning full panoramic views of the Swiss Alps. You can then venture in and casually walk around the old town, take in a vast array of food and drink options, check out the hipster town of Carouge, lay on the beach in Bains Des Paquis, or attend the Plainpalais Market.

You can go fishing on the lake, and it only costs 10 francs to get a fishing license. You can also rent a paddle-board and explore the lake.

Lake Lugano, located in Ticino, sits on the Swiss/Italian border. Italian speakers will get by farther in this Italian canton. Not only will you be tantalizing your taste buds with their variety of citrus, figs, and pomegranates, but you can enjoy it all while staring off to the snowcapped peaks.

The lake is surrounded by beautiful wildflowers in the spring and summer, making it a great destination for hiking and outdoor activities.
Head west to Lake Maggiore and you’ll be trying to figure out if you’ve stepped into Italy itself or head to the capital, Bellinzona, to visit the three UNESCO world heritage site castles.

Lake Thun is one of the two stunning lakes that the city of Interlaken is sandwiched between.

The lake is a great destination for outdoor activities and is surrounded by beautiful scenery.
Out of all the brunch spots in Geneva, this might be the most famous. The buffet offers endless options with quiches, pastries, eggs, and more.

This French cuisine is based on the tradition of French gourmet cuisine. Much like Cafe de la Place, the menu tends to change based on the seasons. Plus they have a very wide variety of wine options if you are a wine lover.

A city I traveled from to get to Bern.

A city in Switzerland that I traveled from to get to Bern.

It may be quite a surprise, but Switzerland has three separate regions: the French side, the German side, and the Italian side — each one bordering the respective country, and in each region you’ll find distinct differences. Lake Lugano, located in Ticino, sits on the Swiss/Italian border.
The Läderach Chocolate Experience offers you the opportunity to make your own chocolate while tasting delicious samples along the way.

When eating in Bern, your options will be both local and international. You can find your local options at Lötschberg, throw down some Italian cuisine at Pizza Ristorante Thurm Molino, or try the food and long cocktail list at Kornhaus Café.
However, if you’re not into cheese but would still like to visit the 2000 resident town, you can also visit the Maison Cailler Chocolate factory, or hike the Gorges de la Jogne and Mont Moleson.
Maison Tavel is one of the highlights of Old Town Geneva.

The Mandarin Oriental Geneva is situated right on the Rhone River, it's only 5-minutes away from Old Town and the cultural and touristic districts, and has an on-site Michelin star Indian restaurant.

Staying at Mandarin Oriental Geneva

The hotel has a unique history, being the first hotel built in Europe after the Second World War. It offers 189 fully renovated elegant rooms, 27 of which are suites. The highlight of the hotel is its prime location, 5 minutes away from Geneva's historic Old Town, cultural, and touristic districts. The hotel features a 24-hour fitness center, saunas, and a full-service beauty salon. The rooms include a flat-screen television, bold modern art, speakers for music, and a Nespresso coffee bar. The staff has access to the mini-fridges from the hallway, allowing them to restock the room without disturbing the guests.
When in Geneva, picnic! Head to Migros grocery and pick up various types of chocolate and cheese. It’s a low-cost way to try a large variety of these selections.
The town hosts the Montreux Jazz Festival in July and is a great destination for music lovers.
One of the dramatic mountains surrounding Interlaken.
The town hosts the Paleo music festival in July and is a great destination for music lovers.
Oberhofen is a fairytale castle perched on the lakeside of Lake Brienz.

In short, so many people have had their hands on the ownership of this 13th-century castle, that when they decided to continuously add new rooms it became a whirlwind of styles. Think a mix of baroque, romantic, and exotic Prussian styles…it’s certainly something! The castle also includes a library, smoking room, as well as a museum that showcases the feudal societies that called it home between the 16th and 19th centuries.
The Old Arsenal is one of the highlights of Old Town Geneva.

The Old City is straight out of a storybook, surrounded by a turquoise river. A scenic walk up to the Rose Garden gives you a view over the Old Town- that looks just like a medieval city surrounded by a moat!
A historic area in Bern that I explored during my stay. The city is truly a fairytale and I fell head over heels in love with it.

Old Town Geneva offers everything from a stunning cathedral to a number of unusual art galleries, some highlights include Cathedral St. Pierre, La Place du Bourg-de-Four, Maison Tavel, and the Old Arsenal.

A center for planning activities such as night sledding, intro-to-snowboarding classes, paragliding, and more.

Outdoor Interlaken is the hub for adrenaline-inducing activities in Interlaken, offering all types of tours, guides for snow sports, and gear rentals.
The Palace of Nations houses the United Nations Office, and the best way to see it is to take a guided tour, proof of identity is needed for the tour so pack your passport.
Parc de la Grange is a park located near the Mandarin Oriental Geneva. It is one of the many attractions that can be accessed from the hotel.

Visit the Physics Museum if you have extra time in your Geneva itinerary.

The CERN museum in Geneva has multiple exhibitions like the Universe of Particles and Microcosm. Though it is most famous for its big bang experiment.
Piz Gloria is the world's first revolving restaurant, offering a lunch experience like no other.

When eating in Bern, your options will be both local and international. You can find your local options at Lötschberg, throw down some Italian cuisine at Pizza Ristorante Thurm Molino, or try the food and long cocktail list at Kornhaus Café.

Explore the Plainpalais market if you have extra time in your Geneva itinerary.

In South Geneva, you will find Plainpalais, a large open space which is known for its flea and farmers markets. The large farmers market happens every Sunday, from early morning until about 5 PM.

Visit the Pointe de la Jonction if you have extra time in your Geneva itinerary.

The Pointe de la Jonction is where the Rhone and Arve rivers meet in Geneva. The waters of the rivers are two different colors (brown and green), and when they come together at the junction, their waters mix together in a beautiful and mesmerizing way.

Rasoi by Vineet is the first fine dining Indian restaurant in Geneva. The restaurant has vibrant red and black decor and serves contemporary Indian cuisine. It is known for its grilled curry leaf-ginger lobster. The restaurant is a great spot for a fine-dining meal.

Visit the Red Cross Museum if you have extra time in your Geneva itinerary.

The International Red Cross Museum first opened up in 1988 and is one of the top museums in Geneva. The exhibitions are very modern rather than traditional and it makes for a very unique experience.

Placed right next to a quiet beach, you’ll find meals made up of local fish, meats, veggies, and fruit. If you’re visiting during the warmer months, be sure to grab a spot outside to soak in the gorgeous views. Also be sure to try the meringues with cream. They are delicious!

The Rhône River is a river that runs through Geneva, offering beautiful views and a scenic backdrop for the city. The Mandarin Oriental Geneva is located near the river, providing guests with easy access to its banks.
Then pick from the 50 museums and 100 art galleries to visit: the top picks being the Kunsthaus Zurich or the Rietberg Museum.

A river that runs through Bern and offers beautiful views from the hotel room balcony.

A river in Bern that offered a beautiful view from my hotel room balcony. The view drifted over the river to the distant Bernese Alps and was breathtaking.
The town is known for its beautiful wildflowers and is a great destination for hiking and outdoor activities.
Definitely, do not miss the sunset from the Rose Garden. I set my alarm the following morning before sunrise. It was one of the most beautiful things I’ve ever seen.
Schilthorn Mountain offers jaw-dropping views of the UNESCO World Heritage trio—Eiger, Mönch, and Jungfrau mountains.

place: The Ritz-Carlton Hotel de la Paix
description: Once a favorite of Grace Kelly, this smaller lakeside hotel was revamped and given a very contemporary and trendy feel without losing all the historic features. It features 75 rooms and an in-hotel chocolate shop.
The region is a great destination for outdoor activities and is surrounded by beautiful scenery, with little to no snow in the winter.

The Tiffany Hotel is located in Geneva's art district, just a 10-minute walk to Lake Geneva, and is the perfect choice if you're looking for a boutique hotel that's close enough to attractions and anything else you might need.

Hidden away in Geneva’s art district, the Tiffany Hotel is just a 10-minute stroll to Lake Geneva. There is also a selection of cafes and bars that are within walking distance. The hotel is Art Nouveau style—brightly colored with peacock hues—and all the rooms are different in shape and size due to the building getting narrower towards the roof.

I had spent the summer traveling around Europe and needed to get more pages added to my passport before I flew to Iceland. As I was planning my trip to Iceland, I found the cheapest flight there via Basel, Switzerland. I planned to visit the US embassy in Switzerland to get more passport pages added, then head to Basel to fly out.

A place I visited for an appointment to get more pages added to my passport.

A place I visited during my stay in Bern to get more pages added to my passport.
The Zurich Zoo is great for families, and you can get a view of the city and countryside on the train ride to Uetliberg Mountain.
Make your own swiss army knife at Victorinox, you need to book months in advance.
The town is a great destination for outdoor activities and is surrounded by beautiful scenery.
A hostel located at Interlaken Ost station, offering a fun and comfy stay for travelers.
The town is a great destination for skiers and snowboarders, but can be quiet in the off-season.

To my surprise, the capital of Switzerland is not Zurich. It’s Bern.

The city has a lot to offer — you can visit the fabulous restaurants, enjoy an artsy day at the museum, stroll the old town and just see as much of the city as you can. The best part, it’s perfect to walk! Walking tours are available, or you can venture out on your own.

The city hosts the Street Parade in August and is a great destination for partygoers.
The Zurich Zoo is great for families, and you can get a view of the city and countryside on the train ride to Uetliberg Mountain.
